verdict.answer_first: B is cleaner but neither gives the fix in sentence 1; B starts with the core diagnosis: "main problem is an N+1 query run sequentially".
verdict.decisiveness: B is less option-heavy, but still conditional; A explicitly surveys: "Two fixes, best first" then says "even better", which weakens the recommendation.
verdict.fabrication: A mildly over-infers ORM: "looks like Sequelize"; B also hedges from API shape: "Sequelize-style, which the API suggests". No hard fabricated numbers; B's "100 orders that's 101" is derived from code.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the batched Map fix, then one ORM join note, then one scale warning: "findAll() with no limit loads the whole table into memory".
